title: Why Didnt Gendo Stop the Dummy Plug Before Toji Was Hurt
display_title: Why Didnt Gendo Stop the Dummy Plug Before Toji Was Hurt
wikitext:
[]: Why Didn't Gendo Stop the Dummy Plug Before Toji Was Hurt?             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Aug 10, 2008 2:35 pm In episode 18, when the Dummy Plug is used for the first time, it is quite successful at stopping Bardiel. However, Gendo didn't order ejection of the Dummy Plug once the Angel was dead. He simply watched as Unit 01 ripped Unit 03 apart, clearly traumatizing his son. Even as Toji's entry plug was about to be crushed, he simply watched. Why? What could he possibly gain by traumatizing his son and knowingly injuring (and killing, for all he knew at the time) the Fourth Child(ren)? Furthermore, if he could have kept Unit 03 at least somewhat in tact, it would have been beneficial to him, as it would have gone under Misato's jurisdiction. Even if Unit 03 were completely unsalvageable, however, and he was waiting out of curiosity as to what the Dummy Plug would do if left alone, deliberately letting Unit 01 crush a child whom Gendo knew was Shinji's classmate seems sadistic even for him, and I can't think of a single way it would help Gendo with any of his plans for Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sun Aug 10, 2008 2:43 pm Maybe he just is that evil.... But the Dummy System''s orders are to defeat the angel, the plug would be the command center for the Eva. But once the plug was destroyed it just shut down with no orders or anything so....

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Aug 10, 2008 3:04 pm I think he really did want to traumatize Shinji, specifically for the purpose of driving him away but also as punishment for not obeying orders. He didn't want him there to begin with, remember, and now that his shiny new dummy plug system has proven successful he doesn't need the little whiner stinking up the place.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Aug 10, 2008 3:06 pm ITT sweeping misconceptions of Gendo's character.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Sun Aug 10, 2008 4:00 pm He wanted to test the dummy plug system, and that was his chance. Also, more than likely Gendo thought that the 4th child was expendable, because he (Toji) was really never part of his plan, so it would have been no loss to him.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Aug 10, 2008 4:14 pm Gendo's an ass. Nuff said.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Aug 10, 2008 4:50 pm Cause Bardiel was in the freaking plug. It's been said I don't know how many times.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sun Aug 10, 2008 5:00 pm Well now it's been said once more which greatly helps those who didn't know that (such as myself).

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Aug 10, 2008 6:01 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Cause Bardiel was in the freaking plug. It's been said I don't know how many times.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Aug 10, 2008 6:04 pm NAveryW wrote: In ? How did it get inside the plug from that cloud?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Aug 10, 2008 6:14 pm

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Aug 10, 2008 6:28 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote:

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sun Aug 10, 2008 7:39 pm 1. Toji himself could have been infected, or even already dead for all they knew. 2. As others have noted, the angel was in the plug. 3. It's been well-established that Gendo is a world-class prick.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Sun Aug 10, 2008 7:49 pm chee wrote: 1. Toji himself could have been infected, or even already dead for all they knew.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Aug 10, 2008 8:06 pm You can actually see a bunch of Bardiel strands connected to Toji, and the Entry Plug bleeds blood (not LCL) when it gets crushed. There was probably a large mass of the Angel occupying the plug right next to Toji, and that mass needed to be squashed to end any further infection. And when Gendo smiles, a small part of his mind, deep inside, is crying.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sun Aug 10, 2008 8:09 pm You think Toji went through the same crap Rei did in 23 or Shinji in 16?

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Sun Aug 10, 2008 8:15 pm ^ Na, he just lost his leg. That can easily be replaced with the med tech in Eva: good grief, they replaced Keel Lorentz spine with a metal prosthetic! The only other sci-fi I know of that can do that is Star Wars*, and that series has a history of space travel that stretches over ten thousand years! *Vader had a metal spine that you could see when he got zapped in Return of the Jedi

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 10, 2008 8:29 pm You think Toji went through the same crap Rei did in 23 or Shinji in 16?

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sun Aug 10, 2008 8:31 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Though don't forget about Asuka in episode 22' (doll Asuka=Arael).

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Aug 10, 2008 8:44 pm But if it's a fungus, how exactly does crushing the plug kill it?

Even as Toji's entry plug was about to be crushed, he simply watched. Why? What could he possibly gain by traumatizing his son and knowingly injuring (and killing, for all he knew at the time) the Fourth Child(ren)? Furthermore, if he could have kept Unit 03 at least somewhat in tact, it would have been beneficial to him, as it would have gone under Misato's jurisdiction.
